title: Samut Sakhon province

infobox.value: ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): สมุทรสาคร, pronounced [[sā.mùt sǎː.kʰɔ̄ːn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Thai "Help:IPA/Thai")) is one of the central [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_Thailand "Provinces of Thailand") (*changwat*) of [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"), located along the coast of the Gulf of Thailand. In 2024, it had a population of 590,867,[[2]](#cite_note-DOPA-2) and an area of 866 km2 (334 sq mi),[[1]](#cite_note-AREA-1) making it the 43rd most populated province whilst being the 4th smallest.

**Samut Sakhon** ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): สมุทรสาคร, pronounced [[sā.mùt sǎː.kʰɔ̄ːn]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Thai "Help:IPA/Thai")) is one of the central [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_Thailand "Provinces of Thailand") (*changwat*) of [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"), located along the coast of the Gulf of Thailand. In 2024, it had a population of 590,867,[[2]](#cite_note-DOPA-2) and an area of 866 km2 (334 sq mi),[[1]](#cite_note-AREA-1) making it the 43rd most populated province whilst being the 4th smallest.

## Toponymy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Toponymy")]

The word *samut* originates from the [Sanskrit](/wiki/Sanskrit "Sanskrit") word *samudra* meaning 'ocean', and the word *sakhon* from Sanskrit *sagara* meaning 'lake'.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Neighboring provinces are (from the southwest clockwise) [Samut Songkhram](/wiki/Samut_Songkhram_province "Samut Songkhram province"), [Ratchaburi](/wiki/Ratchaburi_province "Ratchaburi province"), [Nakhon Pathom](/wiki/Nakhon_Pathom_province "Nakhon Pathom province"), and [Bangkok](/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok"). Samut Sakhon is part of the [Bangkok Metropolitan Region](/wiki/Bangkok_Metropolitan_Region "Bangkok Metropolitan Region").  
Samut Sakhon is at the mouth of the [Tha Chin River](/wiki/Tha_Chin_River "Tha Chin River"), a distributary of the [Chao Phraya River](/wiki/Chao_Phraya_River "Chao Phraya River"), to the [Gulf of Thailand](/wiki/Gulf_of_Thailand "Gulf of Thailand"). At the coast are many salt pans used for harvesting [sea salt](/wiki/Sea_salt "Sea salt").[[5]](#cite_note-SALT-5) The total forest area is 42 km2 (16 sq mi) or 4.9 percent of provincial area.[[1]](#cite_note-AREA-1)

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Climate")]

Samut Sakhon province has a [tropical savanna climate](/wiki/Tropical_savanna_climate "Tropical savanna climate") ([Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification") category Aw). Winters are dry and warm. Temperatures rise until May. The monsoon season runs from May through October, with heavy rain and somewhat cooler temperatures during the day, although nights remain warm. Climatological data for the period 1981–2010: Maximum temperature is 39.7 °C (103.5 °F) in April and May and the lowest temperature is 12.0 °C (53.6 °F) in December. The highest average temperature is 35.4 °C (95.7 °F) in April and the minimum average temperature is 22.0 °C (71.6 °F) in December. Mean annual rainfall is 1648 millimeters. The maximum daily rainfall is 248 millimeters in May. Mean rainy days average 130 days per year.[[6]](#cite_note-CLIMATE-6)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: History")]

The oldest name of the area is *Tha Chin* ('Chinese pier'), probably referring to the fact that it was a trading port where Chinese [junks](/wiki/Junk_(ship) "Junk (ship)") arrived.[[7]](#cite_note-THACHIN-7) In 1548 the city Sakhon Buri was established, and was renamed *Mahachai* in 1704 after the [Khlong Mahachai](/wiki/Khlong_Mahachai "Khlong Mahachai") which was dug then and connected with the Tha Chin River near the town. King [Mongkut](/wiki/Mongkut "Mongkut") gave it its current name, but the old name *Mahachai* is still sometimes used by locals.[[8]](#cite_note-MAHACHAI-8)
It was established by the [Act Establishing Changwat Samut Prakan, Changwat Nonthaburi, Changwat Samut Sakhon, and Changwat Nakhon Nayok, Buddhist Era 2489 (1946)](https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Act_Establishing_Changwat_Samut_Prakan,_Changwat_Nonthaburi,_Changwat_Samut_Sakhon,_and_Changwat_Nakhon_Nayok,_Buddhist_Era_2489_(1946) "s:Act Establishing Changwat Samut Prakan, Changwat Nonthaburi, Changwat Samut Sakhon, and Changwat Nakhon Nayok, Buddhist Era 2489 (1946)"), which came into force on 9 May 1946.[[9]](#cite_note-PROVINCE-9)

## Economy and environment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Economy and environment")]

Formerly an agricultural- and fisheries-based province, Samut Sakhon in 2020 has more than 6,000 factories, most of them small, employing fewer than 50 workers, and too small to warrant much attention from Thailand's Pollution Control Department (PCD). Small firms lack the budgets to install the environmental gear that would help protect the environment. As a result, Samut Sakhon is one of the most polluted provinces in the nation.[[10]](#cite_note-POLLUTION-10)

Soil and water samples from the industrial area of Mueang District were found to be contaminated with high levels of arsenic, lead, cadmium, chromium, zinc, copper, and nickel. High levels of [persistent organic pollutants](/wiki/Persistent_organic_pollutants "Persistent organic pollutants") (POPS), byproducts of industrial processes, were present in eggs from free-range chickens. An egg tested by researchers was found to have 84 [nanograms](/wiki/Nanogram "Nanogram") per kilogram of [dioxins](/wiki/Polychlorinated_dibenzodioxins "Polychlorinated dibenzodioxins") and [furans](/wiki/Polychlorinated_dibenzofurans "Polychlorinated dibenzofurans"), a level 33 times higher than the safety limit observed by the European Union.[[10]](#cite_note-POLLUTION-10)

The most polluted air in Thailand in 2018 was found to be in Samut Sakhon province.[[11]](#cite_note-MOSTPOLLUTED-11) According to the PCD, the level of [PM2.5](/wiki/Particulates "Particulates") in the provincial atmosphere in 2019 was unusually high, measuring as high as 195 [micrograms](/wiki/Microgram "Microgram") per cubic metre (μg/m3). During the air pollution "season" of 2018–2019, PM2.5 levels exceeded the PCD's safe threshold of 50 μg/m3 for 41 days.[[10]](#cite_note-POLLUTION-10)

Samut Sakhon is a leading sea salt producer. According to a survey in 2011, 12,572 [rai](/wiki/Rai_(area) "Rai (area)") of salt pans were managed by 242 families in Samut Sakhon.[[5]](#cite_note-SALT-5)

The number of factories in 2022 was 6,458 with a workforce of 372,282 people.[[12]](#cite_note-FACTORIES-12)

## Health

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Health")]

### Hospitals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Hospitals")]

[Samut Sakhon Hospital](/wiki/Samut_Sakhon_Hospital "Samut Sakhon Hospital") with 600 beds, is the main hospital of Samut Sakhon province, operated by the [Ministry of Public Health](/wiki/Ministry_of_Public_Health_(Thailand) "Ministry of Public Health (Thailand)").  
In Mueang district there are also: [Ekachai Hospital](/wiki/List_of_hospitals_in_Thailand#Private_Hospitals "List of hospitals in Thailand") with 142 beds, [Mahachai 3 Hospital](/wiki/List_of_hospitals_in_Thailand#Private_Hospitals "List of hospitals in Thailand") with 180 beds, [Jesada Vechakarn Hospital](/wiki/List_of_hospitals_in_Thailand#Private_Hospitals "List of hospitals in Thailand") with 10 beds and [Metropolitan Hospital Tha Chalom](/wiki/List_of_hospitals_in_Thailand#Community_Hospitals_(Category_M2,_F1,_F2_and_F3) "List of hospitals in Thailand").  
In Krathum Baen district operates the ministry a general hospital, [Krathum Baen Hospital](/wiki/Krathum_Baen_Hospital "Krathum Baen Hospital") with 300 beds.  
Ban Phaeo district is served by [Ban Phaeo General Hospital](/wiki/Ban_Phaeo_General_Hospital "Ban Phaeo General Hospital") with 323 beds, the only hospital public organisation in Thailand.

### Health promoting hospitals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Health promoting hospitals")]

There are total fifty-four [health-promoting hospitals](/wiki/Hospitals_in_Thailand#Sub-district_Health_Promoting_Hospitals "Hospitals in Thailand") in the province, of which; twenty-three in Mueang district, twelve in Krathum Baen district and nineteen in Ban Phaeo district.[[13]](#cite_note-HOSPITALS-13)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Education")]

* Krathum Baen district - 32 schools
* Ban Phaeo district - 36 schools

### Religion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Religion")]

There are one hundred sixteen [Theravada](/wiki/Theravada "Theravada") Buddhist temples in the province.  
Seventy in Mueang district, fifteen in Krathum Baen district and thirty-one in Ban Phaeo district.[[17]](#cite_note-MAHANIKAI-17)[[18]](#cite_note-DHAMMAYUT-18)

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Transportation")]

The province is the intersection of [highway 35](/wiki/Rama_II_Road "Rama II Road") eastbound (Bangkok route), highway 35 westbound ([Pak Tho district](/wiki/Pak_Tho_district "Pak Tho district"), [Ratchaburi province](/wiki/Ratchaburi_province "Ratchaburi province")), Ekkachai road (no. 3242) ([Chom Thong district](/wiki/Chom_Thong_district,_Bangkok "Chom Thong district, Bangkok"), Bangkok), [highway 4](/wiki/Phetkasem_Road "Phetkasem Road") eastbound (Bangkok route), south/west bound (southern Thailand).  
The area is served by some fifteen bus lines.[[19]](#cite_note-ROADS-19)  
Samut Sakhon is along the [Maeklong Railway](/wiki/Maeklong_Railway "Maeklong Railway"), operated by the [State Railway of Thailand](/wiki/State_Railway_of_Thailand "State Railway of Thailand") and is served by [Mahachai railway station](/wiki/Mahachai_railway_station "Mahachai railway station") on the east bank of the Tha Chin River and [Ban Laem railway station](/wiki/Ban_Laem_railway_station "Ban Laem railway station") on the west bank.

## Symbols

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Samut_Sakhon_province&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Symbols")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4c/Provincial_Brand.png/120px-Provincial_Brand.png)](/wiki/File:Provincial_Brand.png)

Brand

The [provincial seal](/wiki/Seals_of_the_provinces_of_Thailand "Seals of the provinces of Thailand") shows a [Chinese junk](/wiki/Chinese_junk "Chinese junk") in front of the coast, with a factory and a smoking chimney in the background. Both refer to the old trading tradition as well as the local industries.

The [provincial flag](/wiki/List_of_Thai_flags#Provincial_flags "List of Thai flags") is horizontally divided pink/light blue/pink (1:3:1) the provincial seal in the middle.

The provincial brand is a picture of a white factory, a fishing boat, a fish and blue water and a green leaf.

The [provincial tree](/wiki/List_of_Thai_provincial_trees "List of Thai provincial trees") is commonly called blackboard tree or devil's tree (*[Alstonia scholaris](/wiki/Alstonia_scholaris "Alstonia scholaris")*).

The provincial aquatic animal is the [short mackerel](/wiki/Short_mackerel "Short mackerel") (*[Rastrelliger brachysoma](/wiki/Rastrelliger_brachysoma "Rastrelliger brachysoma")*) what with Samut Sakhon is the first province to have short mackerel fishing boat and has the largest amount of this species of fish caught in Thailand.

The provincial [slogan](/wiki/Slogan "Slogan") is "Fishing city, factory town, agricultural ground, historic site".[[20]](#cite_note-SYMBOLS-20)
